description_long: This sculpture depicts a mythological female figure with a human upper body and a fish-like lower body, reminiscent of a mermaid or siren. The figure is seated on a rectangular plinth, with her arms bent and one hand resting on her tail. Her facial features are finely detailed, and she wears a serene expression. The tail curves upward and splits into two, with the tips curling outward. Atop her head is a blocky, rectangular form that appears to be a stylized headdress or possibly a damaged or missing element. The surface of the sculpture is textured and weathered, suggesting it is made of stone or concrete.
description: A weathered stone sculpture of a mermaid-like female figure seated on a plinth, with a split, curling tail and a blocky form atop her head.
description_view_0: Front view showing the detailed face, upper torso, and both arms of the mermaid figure, with the split tail and blocky headpiece clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curvature of the tail and the profile of the head and blocky form, with one arm and the plinth visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back, the full length of the tail, and the attachment to the plinth, with the head and blocky form seen from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other curve of the tail, the back of the head, and the blocky form, with the arm and torso in profile.
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background and surroundings are not shown in the images.
